destinations.evidence: During payload construction for a parent header, the port sets current_block to parent_header.number + 1, computes Scheduler obligations for that block from parent state, and iterates every returned obligation to synthesize and enqueue a scheduler-routed PayloadCandidate::Scheduled.
Each obligation is converted into a protocol TxScheduled calling Scheduler.execute(callId,index), preserving the one-obligation-to-one-scheduled-transaction behavior required by the invariant.

equivalence_argument: The old invariant names the synchronous scheduler/canonical callback shape, but the v2.2 port intentionally removed the old crates/scheduling service surface. The effective behavior is preserved in the payload builder: when building block N+1 from parent N, it computes obligations for N+1 from Scheduler parent state, filters them, creates one TxScheduled per obligation, and submits those transactions into the same payload candidate path that later executes scheduler-routed candidates. This preserves due-block selection and per-call scheduled transaction submission, with the callback boundary refactored into payload candidate construction.

related_but_insufficient_code: The old crates/scheduling evidence paths are absent in the port workspace; they are replaced by parent-state obligation synthesis in payload building.

notes: Mapped structurally in the port; the old synchronous scheduler callback surface is absent but its effective submission behavior is implemented in payload construction.
rationale: The low-confidence async CallDue event candidates are superseded by the synchronous scheduler/canonical processing path. The current effective behavior in the bounded packet is that canonical block processing obtains due calls from SynchronousScheduler and converts each due call into a scheduled transaction submission.
statement: When processing canonical block N, the scheduler must compute calls due for block N+1, return those due calls through the synchronous scheduler path, create one scheduled transaction for each returned call, and submit each transaction through the canonical scheduling callback.
suggested_falsification_tests: Set scheduler state with calls due at N, N+1, and N+2, process canonical block N, and assert only the N+1 call is returned and converted into a scheduled transaction.
Process a canonical block with two calls due for N+1 and assert the callback receives exactly two scheduled transactions with the expected call IDs and execution blocks.
summary: The port preserves next-block scheduled transaction synthesis through payload-builder obligation scanning and scheduled candidate insertion.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Build a payload with Scheduler parent state containing two obligations due at parent+1 and one due at parent+2; assert only the parent+1 obligations become scheduler-routed PayloadCandidate::Scheduled transactions that call Scheduler.execute with the expected call IDs and indices.
title: Canonical scheduler submits next-block due calls
